questions.q: The wall of the GI tract, from inner to outer, consists of which four layers?
options: C. Mucosa, submucosa, muscularis, serosa (or adventitia)

questions.q: The enteric nervous system consists of which two major plexuses?
options: E. Submucosal and autonomic plexuses only

questions.q: Which plexus of the enteric nervous system is located between the inner circular and outer longitudinal smooth muscle layers and primarily controls GI motility?
The four regions of the stomach from proximal (esophageal end) to distal (duodenal end) are:
options: B. Cardia, fundus, body, pyloric region (antrum)

questions.q: The stomach is unique among GI organs in having a third muscle layer. What is it?
options: C. Oblique layer (innermost; in addition to circular and longitudinal)

questions.q: The mucosal folds of the empty or distended stomach are called:
Chief cells of the gastric glands secrete:
options: A. Hydrochloric acid and intrinsic factor

questions.q: Parietal cells of the gastric glands secrete which two products?
options: B. Hydrochloric acid (HCl) and intrinsic factor

questions.q: The hormone gastrin, secreted by G cells of the stomach, primarily stimulates:
options: A. Bicarbonate secretion from the pancreas
C. Secretion of HCl by parietal cells
E. Insulin release from the pancreas

questions.q: Secretin, released from S cells of the duodenum in response to acidic chyme, primarily stimulates:
options: A. HCl secretion by parietal cells
B. Gallbladder contraction and pancreatic enzyme release
C. Pancreatic and biliary bicarbonate secretion to neutralize acidic chyme
D. Increased gastric motility and emptying
E. Mucus secretion by goblet cells only

questions.q: Which structural adaptation of the small intestine consists of permanent transverse folds visible to the naked eye that increase surface area ~3-fold?
Within each intestinal villus, the central lymphatic capillary responsible for absorbing dietary fat is called the:
options: E. Central vein of the villus

questions.q: Brunner's glands (duodenal glands), found in the submucosa of the duodenum, secrete:
options: D. Alkaline mucus to protect the duodenum from acidic chyme

questions.q: The sphincter that controls the flow of bile and pancreatic secretions into the duodenum at the ampulla of Vater is the:
The three longitudinal bands of smooth muscle along the outer surface of the large intestine are called:
The sacculations (pouches) of the large intestine wall, formed by the contraction of the taeniae coli, are called:
The liver is divided into right and left lobes by the:
A hepatic portal triad consists of which three structures running together in the liver?
options: A. Hepatic artery, hepatic vein, and bile duct
B. Branch of the portal vein, branch of the hepatic artery, and bile duct
C. Hepatocytes, sinusoids, and Kupffer cells
D. Hepatic vein, portal vein, and gallbladder duct
E. Central vein, sinusoids, and portal triad

questions.q: Which cells lining the hepatic sinusoids serve as resident macrophages, phagocytizing debris, old RBCs, and pathogens from portal blood?
Which of the following is NOT a function of hepatocytes?
options: C. Synthesis of plasma proteins (albumin, fibrinogen, clotting factors)
D. Detoxification of drugs and alcohol
E. Production of digestive enzymes (amylase, lipase) for the small intestine

questions.q: Dietary fats (long-chain triglycerides) are absorbed from the intestinal wall into:
options: A. Directly into blood capillaries as fatty acids and glycerol
C. The portal vein as intact triglycerides
D. Lymph as free fatty acids only
E. Via carrier-mediated transport into portal capillaries

questions.q: Salivary amylase begins digesting which macromolecule in the oral cavity?
Pepsinogen is activated to the active enzyme pepsin by:
options: B. Hydrochloric acid (HCl) in the stomach

questions.q: Bile's primary role in fat digestion is:
options: A. Chemically breaking down triglycerides (enzymatic digestion)
C. Emulsifying fat globules into smaller droplets to increase surface area for lipase action
E. Converting fatty acids to chylomicrons within the intestinal lumen

questions.q: The intestinal enzyme enterokinase (enteropeptidase) activates which pancreatic proenzyme, which then activates other pancreatic proenzymes?
The terminal ileum is the only segment specialized for absorption of:
options: C. Vitamin B12 (cobalamin) bound to intrinsic factor
E. Fat-soluble vitamins A, D, E, K

questions.q: Gastric inhibitory peptide (GIP), released from the small intestine in response to glucose and fat, primarily acts to:
options: A. Increase gastric motility and acid secretion
B. Stimulate HCl secretion by parietal cells
C. Inhibit gastric activity and stimulate insulin release from the pancreas
E. Increase bile production by hepatocytes

questions.q: The submandibular salivary gland:
options: A. Is the smallest of the three major salivary glands
C. Is located below the ear
D. Produces mixed serous and mucous secretions and contributes the majority of resting saliva volume

questions.q: The layer of the GI tract wall that contains the submucosal (Meissner's) nerve plexus and blood and lymphatic vessels is the:
The large intestine functions primarily to:
options: A. Perform chemical digestion of carbohydrates not absorbed in the small intestine
B. Absorb most of the ingested dietary proteins
C. Reabsorb water and electrolytes and compact remaining material into feces
D. Produce bile for fat emulsification
E. Absorb fat-soluble vitamins from bile micelles

questions.q: Finger-like projections of the intestinal mucosa that dramatically increase absorptive surface area are called:
The brush border (microvilli) of intestinal enterocytes contains membrane-bound enzymes that complete the final digestion of:
options: A. Bile salts and fat micelles
C. Disaccharides (maltose, sucrose, lactose) and short peptides

questions.q: Which of the following correctly describes bile?
options: A. An enzyme produced by the pancreas
B. A secretion produced by the liver, stored in the gallbladder, and released into the duodenum to emulsify fat
C. Produced by the gallbladder in response to CCK stimulation
D. An enzyme that chemically digests fats into fatty acids and glycerol
E. A secretion containing pepsin and amylase

questions.q: Fat-filled pouches (fatty tabs) attached to the outer surface of the large intestine are called:
The ileocecal valve controls the passage of material from the:
options: D. Cecum to the ascending colon

questions.q: The organ that continuously produces approximately 1 liter of bile per day (even without a meal) is the:
Protein digestion in the stomach is initiated by pepsin acting on:
options: C. Polypeptides and proteins, cleaving peptide bonds
E. Only short peptides, not intact proteins

questions.q: The liver's two smaller anatomical lobes (in addition to the large right and left lobes) are the:
Blood flow through the liver sinusoids travels from the portal triad toward the:
options: C. Central vein (which drains into the hepatic veins → inferior vena cava)

questions.q: Intrinsic factor, secreted by parietal cells of the stomach, is required for:
options: A. Activation of pepsinogen to pepsin
B. Neutralization of acidic chyme in the duodenum
C. Absorption of vitamin B12 in the terminal ileum

questions.q: Which is the correct sequence of carbohydrate digestion from ingestion to absorption?
options: A. Pancreatic lipase → salivary amylase → glucose
B. Salivary amylase → pancreatic amylase → brush border disaccharidases → monosaccharides (glucose, galactose, fructose)
D. Gastric acid → amylase → sucrase → fructose
E. Brush border enzymes → salivary amylase → glucose
